stories.headline: Can Trump finally make a nuclear deal with Iran?
stories.summary: After going to war twice, the US president is again trying to strike a deal on the regime’s uranium enrichment programme

stories.headline: Trump Sees Iran Deal as Imminent, Nuclear Issue Remains in Limbo
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said a deal with Iran to end the seven-week war may be imminent though disagreements remain, with Tehran pushing back against US claims that it had agreed to give up its enriched uranium.

stories.headline: Hank Paulson Says the US Will Weather Iran War Fallout Better Than Anyone Else

stories.headline: Iran has learned that the Strait of Hormuz is its strongest deterrent
stories.summary: Donald Heflin, a senior fellow at Tufts University, says Iran has shown it doesn’t need nuclear weapons as a deterrent.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Hormuz Strait shut down again over US ‘piracy’, says Tehran
stories.summary: President Trump says US naval blockade will 'remain in full force' until 'our transaction with Iran is 100%' done.

stories.headline: Iran claims ‘strict control’ of Strait of Hormuz and says it will not be fully reopened
stories.summary: Tehran says Donald Trump ‘raised seven claims in one hour and all seven were false’

stories.headline: Iran Says Strait of Hormuz is Shut Amid Reports of Gunfire
stories.summary: Iran broadcast to ships in the Strait of Hormuz that the vital oil and gas channel is closed to maritime traffic again, with owners reporting gunfire in the waterway less than 24 hours after the nation’s foreign minister declared it open. stories.headline: Lebanon Cease-Fire and Hormuz ‘Opening’ Raise Hopes for Iran Talks, but Hard Work Awaits
stories.summary: President Trump said the process of clinching a final peace deal with Iran would now “go very quickly.” But analysts called that unlikely.

stories.headline: Stocks Jump in Face of Uncertainty Over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon and news that Iran allegedly decided to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping sent risky assets surging anew on Friday. This surge extended a rally that pushed the S&P 500 to a fresh record and fueling its biggest monthly advance since 2020. stories.headline: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again over US blockade of its ports
stories.summary: Reports of Iranian gunboats opening fire on tanker after Tehran says it is closing waterway until US lifts its blockade.

stories.headline: Tankers Test Hormuz Reopening as Uncertainty Lingers Over Strait

stories.headline: Moscow bombards Ukraine while Kyiv targets Russian oil facilities
stories.summary: Ukraine struck multiple Russian oil refineries with drones on Saturday while simultaneously repelling a 219-drone Russian assault on its own territory.

stories.headline: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report

stories.headline: UK Bears Scars to Economy, Trump Ties Even as War Risks Subside

stories.headline: Takeaways From the Supreme Court’s Shadow Papers

stories.headline: Why China is taking a behind-the-scenes role in the Iran war

stories.headline: Is Congress having a reckoning over sexual misconduct – or just a moment?

stories.headline: Lufthansa 100th Birthday Bash Leaves Little Room for Cheers
stories.summary: Deutsche Lufthansa AG’s 100th anniversary bash this week had all the elements of a picture-perfect party. The sun was out, flight attendants in polished vintage uniforms greeted guests, and Chief Executive Officer Carsten Spohr beamed on stage with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz.

stories.headline: Odd Lots: Why Economists Might Be Getting AI Wrong (Podcast)
stories.summary: Everyone knows that new technologies can be really disruptive to the labor market, but eventually new jobs emerge and things come back into balance. And there is a sense in which many view AI with the same lens. Yes, there will be pain in some sectors, but then there will be productivity gains and new sources of demand and new opportunities for labor that we can’t conceive of yet. stories.headline: Australian Minister Says Fuel Reserves Up, More Shipments on Way
stories.summary: Australia’s fuel reserves rose in the past week and more is on the way, Energy Minister Chris Bowen said, easing concern of shortages in the import-reliant country.

stories.headline: Fermi Shares Plunge After Announcing Departure of CEO
stories.summary: Fermi shares fell as much as 31% in post-market trading after the developer behind a massive planned AI campus in Texas announced the immediate departure of co-founder and Chief Executive Officer Toby Neugebauer.
